NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “Do Not Destroy.” A Michtam of David. Do you indeed speak righteousness, you silent ones? Do you judge uprightly, you sons of men?
NKJV.2: No, in heart you work wickedness; You weigh out the violence of your hands in the earth.
NKJV.3: The wicked are estranged from the womb; They go astray as soon as they are born, speaking lies.
NKJV.4: Their poison is like the poison of a serpent; They are like the deaf cobra that stops its ear,
NKJV.5: Which will not heed the voice of charmers, Charming ever so skillfully.
NKJV.6: Break their teeth in their mouth, O God! Break out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD!
NKJV.7: Let them flow away as waters which run continually; When he bends his bow, Let his arrows be as if cut in pieces.
NKJV.8: Let them be like a snail which melts away as it goes, Like a stillborn child of a woman, that they may not see the sun.
NKJV.9: Before your pots can feel the burning thorns, He shall take them away as with a whirlwind, As in His living and burning wrath.
NKJV.10: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance;  He shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked,
NKJV.11: So that men will say, “Surely there is a reward for the righteous; Surely He is God who judges in the earth.”

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, Altaschith<S>516</S>, Michtam<S>4387</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Do ye indeed<S>552</S> speak<S>1696</S> righteousness<S>6664</S>, O congregation<S>482</S>? do ye judge<S>8199</S> uprightly<S>4339</S>, O ye sons<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>? <sup>Altaschith: or, Destroy not, A golden Psalm of David</sup>
KJV.2: Yea, in heart<S>3820</S> ye work<S>6466</S> wickedness<S>5766</S>; ye weigh<S>6424</S> the violence<S>2555</S> of your hands<S>3027</S> in the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.3: The wicked<S>7563</S> are estranged<S>2114</S> from the womb<S>7358</S>: they go astray<S>8582</S> as soon as they be born<S>990</S>, speaking<S>1696</S> lies<S>3577</S>. <sup>as soon: Heb. from the belly</sup>
KJV.4: Their poison<S>2534</S> is like<S>1823</S> the poison<S>2534</S> of a serpent<S>5175</S>: they are like the deaf<S>2795</S> adder<S>6620</S> that stoppeth<S>331</S> her ear<S>241</S>; <sup>like the poison: Heb. according to the likeness, etc</sup> <sup>adder: or, asp</sup>
KJV.5: Which will not hearken<S>8085</S> to the voice<S>6963</S> of charmers<S>3907</S>, charming<S>2266</S> <S>2267</S> never so wisely<S>2449</S>. <sup>charming: or, be the charmer never so cunning</sup>
KJV.6: Break<S>2040</S> their teeth<S>8127</S>, O God<S>430</S>, in their mouth<S>6310</S>: break out<S>5422</S> the great teeth<S>4459</S> of the young lions<S>3715</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>.
KJV.7: Let them melt away<S>3988</S> as waters<S>4325</S> which run continually<S>1980</S>: when he bendeth<S>1869</S> his bow to shoot his arrows<S>2671</S>, let them be as cut in pieces<S>4135</S>.
KJV.8: As a snail<S>7642</S> which melteth<S>8557</S>, let every one of them pass away<S>1980</S>: like the untimely birth<S>5309</S> of a woman<S>802</S>, that they may not see<S>2372</S> the sun<S>8121</S>.
KJV.9: Before your pots<S>5518</S> can feel<S>995</S> the thorns<S>329</S>, he shall take them away as with a whirlwind<S>8175</S>, both living<S>2416</S>, and in his wrath<S>2740</S>. <sup>both: Heb. as living as wrath</sup>
KJV.10: The righteous<S>6662</S> shall rejoice<S>8055</S> when he seeth<S>2372</S> the vengeance<S>5359</S>: he shall wash<S>7364</S> his feet<S>6471</S> in the blood<S>1818</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S>.
KJV.11: So that a man<S>120</S> shall say<S>559</S>, Verily there is a reward<S>6529</S> for the righteous<S>6662</S>: verily he is<S>3426</S> a God<S>430</S> that judgeth<S>8199</S> in the earth<S>776</S>. <sup>a reward: Heb. fruit of the, etc</sup>

ESV.1: Do you indeed decree what is right, you gods?  Do you judge the children of man uprightly?
ESV.2: No, in your hearts you devise wrongs; your hands deal out violence on earth.
ESV.3: The wicked are estranged from the womb; they go astray from birth, speaking lies.
ESV.4: They have venom like the venom of a serpent, like the deaf adder that stops its ear,
ESV.5: so that it does not hear the voice of charmers or of the cunning enchanter.
ESV.6: O God, break the teeth in their mouths; tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD!
ESV.7: Let them vanish like water that runs away; when he aims his arrows, let them be blunted.
ESV.8: Let them be like the snail that dissolves into slime, like the stillborn child who never sees the sun.
ESV.9: Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns, whether green or ablaze, may he sweep them away!
ESV.10: The righteous will rejoice when he sees the vengeance; he will bathe his feet in the blood of the wicked.
ESV.11: Mankind will say, “Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges on earth.”

NASB.1: For the choir director; [set to] Al-tashheth. A Mikhtam of David. Do you indeed speak righteousness, O gods? Do you judge uprightly, O sons of men?
NASB.2: No, in heart you work unrighteousness; On earth you weigh out the violence of your hands.
NASB.3: The wicked are estranged from the womb; These who speak lies go astray from birth.
NASB.4: They have venom like the venom of a serpent; Like a deaf cobra that stops up its ear,
NASB.5: So that it does not hear the voice of charmers, [Or] a skillful caster of spells.
NASB.6: O God, shatter their teeth in their mouth; Break out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
NASB.7: Let them flow away like water that runs off; [When] he aims his arrows, let them be as headless shafts.
NASB.8: [Let them be] as a snail which melts away as it goes along, [Like] the miscarriages of a woman which never see the sun.
NASB.9: Before your pots can feel [the fire of] thorns He will sweep them away with a whirlwind, the green and the burning alike.
NASB.10: The righteous will rejoice when he sees the vengeance; He will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
NASB.11: And men will say, 'Surely there is a reward for the righteous; Surely there is a God who judges on earth!'

NLT.1:
Justice — do you rulers know the meaning of the word?
Do you judge the people fairly?
NLT.2:
No! You plot injustice in your hearts.
You spread violence throughout the land.
NLT.3:
These wicked people are born sinners;
even from birth they have lied and gone their own way.
NLT.4:
They spit venom like deadly snakes;
they are like cobras that refuse to listen,
NLT.5:
ignoring the tunes of the snake charmers,
no matter how skillfully they play.
NLT.6:
Break off their fangs, O God!
Smash the jaws of these lions, O LORD!
NLT.7:
May they disappear like water into thirsty ground.
Make their weapons useless in their hands.
NLT.8:
May they be like snails that dissolve into slime,
like a stillborn child who will never see the sun.
NLT.9:
God will sweep them away, both young and old,
faster than a pot heats over burning thorns.
NLT.10:
The godly will rejoice when they see injustice avenged.
They will wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.
NLT.11:
Then at last everyone will say,
“There truly is a reward for those who live for God;
surely there is a God who judges justly here on earth.”

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David. Do you indeed speak righteousness, silent ones? Do you judge blamelessly, you sons of men?
WEB.2: No, in your heart you plot injustice. You measure out the violence of your hands in the earth.
WEB.3: The wicked go astray from the womb. They are wayward as soon as they are born, speaking lies.
WEB.4: Their poison is like the poison of a snake; like a deaf cobra that stops its ear,
WEB.5: which doesn’t listen to the voice of charmers, no matter how skillful the charmer may be.
WEB.6: Break their teeth, God, in their mouth. Break out the great teeth of the young lions, Yahweh.
WEB.7: Let them vanish like water that flows away. When they draw the bow, let their arrows be made blunt.
WEB.8: Let them be like a snail which melts and passes away, like the stillborn child, who has not seen the sun.
WEB.9: Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns, he will sweep away the green and the burning alike.
WEB.10: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance. He shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked;
WEB.11: so that men shall say, “Most certainly there is a reward for the righteous. Most certainly there is a God who judges the earth.”

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: “Do Not Destroy.” A of David. [1] Do you really speak righteously, you mighty ones? [1]
Do you judge people fairly? ⓛ
CSB17.2:
No, you practice injustice in your hearts;
with your hands you weigh out violence in the land. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
The wicked go astray from the womb;
liars wander about from birth. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
They have venom like the venom of a snake,
like the deaf cobra that stops up its ears, ⓐ
CSB17.5:
that does not listen to the sound of the charmers
who skillfully weave spells. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
God, knock the teeth out of their mouths;
LORD, tear out the young lions’ fangs. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
May they vanish like water that flows by;
may they aim their blunted arrows. [2] ⓓ
CSB17.8:
Like a slug that moves along in slime,
like a woman’s miscarried child,
may they not see the sun. ⓔ
CSB17.9:
Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns —
whether green or burning —
he will sweep them away. [3] ⓕ
CSB17.10:
The righteous one will rejoice
when he sees the retribution; ⓖ
he will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked. ⓗ
CSB17.11:
Then people will say,
“Yes, there is a reward for the righteous!
There is a God who judges on earth!” ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Do Not Destroy." A Mikhtam of David. [A record of memorable thoughts of David]. Do you indeed speak righteousness, O gods (heavenly beings)?
Do you judge fairly, O sons of men?
AMP.2:
No, in your heart you devise wrongdoing;
On earth you deal out the violence of your hands.
AMP.3:
The wicked are estranged from the womb;
These go astray from birth, speaking lies [even twisted partial truths].
AMP.4:
Their poison is like the venom of a serpent;
They are like the deaf horned viper that stops up its ear,
AMP.5:
So that it does not listen to the voice of charmers,
Or of the skillful enchanter casting [cunning] spells.
AMP.6:
O God, break their teeth in their mouth;
Break out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
AMP.7:
Let them flow away like water that runs off;
When he aims his arrows, let them be as headless shafts.
AMP.8:
Let them be as a snail which melts away (secretes slime) as it goes along,
Like the miscarriage of a woman which never sees the sun.
AMP.9:
Before your cooking pots can feel the fire of thorns [burning under them as fuel],
He will sweep them away with a whirlwind, the green and the burning ones alike.
AMP.10:
The [unyieldingly] righteous will rejoice when he sees the vengeance [of God];
He will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
AMP.11:
Men will say, "Surely there is a reward for the righteous;
Surely there is a God who judges on the earth."

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Is this any way to run a country?
Is there an honest politician in the house?
MSG.2:
Behind the scenes you brew cauldrons of evil,
behind closed doors you make deals with demons.
MSG.3:
The wicked crawl from the wrong side of the cradle;
their first words out of the womb are lies.
MSG.4:
Poison, lethal rattlesnake poison,
drips from their forked tongues—
MSG.5:
Deaf to threats, deaf to charm,
decades of wax built up in their ears.
MSG.6:
God, smash their teeth to bits,
leave them toothless tigers.
MSG.7:
Let their lives be buckets of water spilled,
all that's left, a damp stain in the sand.
Let them be trampled grass
worn smooth by the traffic.
MSG.8:
Let them dissolve into snail slime,
be a miscarried fetus that never sees sunlight.
MSG.9:
Before what they cook up is half-done, God,
throw it out with the garbage!
MSG.10:
The righteous will call up their friends
when they see the wicked get their reward,
Serve up their blood in goblets
as they toast one another,
MSG.11:
Everyone cheering, "It's worth it to play by the rules!
God's handing out trophies and tending the earth!"

NIV.1:
Psalm 58
For the director of music. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” Of David. A miktam.
Do you rulers indeed speak justly?
Do you judge uprightly among men?
NIV.2:
No, in your heart you devise injustice,
and your hands mete out violence on the earth.
NIV.3:
Even from birth the wicked go astray;
from the womb they are wayward and speak lies.
NIV.4:
Their venom is like the venom of a snake,
like that of a cobra that has stopped its ears,
NIV.5:
that will not heed the tune of the charmer,
however skillful the enchanter may be.
NIV.6:
Break the teeth in their mouths, O God;
tear out, O Lord, the fangs of the lions!
NIV.7:
Let them vanish like water that flows away;
when they draw the bow, let their arrows be blunted.
NIV.8:
Like a slug melting away as it moves along,
like a stillborn child, may they not see the sun.
NIV.9:
Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns—
whether they be green or dry—the wicked will be swept away.
NIV.10:
The righteous will be glad when they are avenged,
when they bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
NIV.11:
Then men will say,
“Surely the righteous still are rewarded;
surely there is a God who judges the earth.”

NRSVCE.1:
Do you indeed decree what is right, you gods?
Do you judge people fairly?
NRSVCE.2:
No, in your hearts you devise wrongs;
your hands deal out violence on earth.
NRSVCE.3:
The wicked go astray from the womb;
they err from their birth, speaking lies.
NRSVCE.4:
They have venom like the venom of a serpent,
like the deaf adder that stops its ear,
NRSVCE.5:
so that it does not hear the voice of charmers
or of the cunning enchanter.
NRSVCE.6:
O God, break the teeth in their mouths;
tear out the fangs of the young lions, O LORD!
NRSVCE.7:
Let them vanish like water that runs away;
like grass let them be trodden down and wither.
NRSVCE.8:
Let them be like the snail that dissolves into slime;
like the untimely birth that never sees the sun.
NRSVCE.9:
Sooner than your pots can feel the heat of thorns,
whether green or ablaze, may he sweep them away!
NRSVCE.10:
The righteous will rejoice when they see vengeance done;
they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
NRSVCE.11:
People will say, “Surely there is a reward for the righteous;
surely there is a God who judges on earth.”

NET.1: Do you rulers really pronounce just decisions? Do you judge people fairly?
NET.2: No! You plan how to do what is unjust; you deal out violence in the earth.
NET.3: The wicked turn aside from birth; liars go astray as soon as they are born.
NET.4: Their venom is like that of a snake, like a deaf serpent that does not hear,
NET.5: that does not respond to the magicians, or to a skilled snake-charmer.
NET.6: O God, break the teeth in their mouths! Smash the jawbones of the lions, O LORD!
NET.7: Let them disappear like water that flows away! Let them wither like grass!
NET.8: Let them be like a snail that melts away as it moves along! Let them be like stillborn babies that never see the sun!
NET.9: Before the kindling is even placed under your pots, he will sweep it away along with both the raw and cooked meat.
NET.10: The godly will rejoice when they see vengeance carried out; they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
NET.11: Then observers will say, “Yes indeed, the godly are rewarded! Yes indeed, there is a God who judges in the earth!”
